questions.q: An organisation is established for the benefit of the community. Which of the following is not a characteristic of a not-for-profit organisation?
opts: (A) Limitation of liability
(B) No distribution of surplus
(C) Tax exemption
(D) Unlimited liability
questions.explanation: A not-for-profit organisation is a type of organisation that is established for the benefit of the community and is not driven by profit motive. Unlimited liability is a characteristic of a company, not a not-for-profit organisation.
questions.topic: Introduction to Not-for-Profit Organisations

questions.q: What is the main objective of a not-for-profit organisation?
opts: (A) To earn maximum profits
(B) To provide services to the community
(C) To distribute surplus among members
(D) To generate employment
questions.explanation: The main objective of a not-for-profit organisation is to provide services to the community, not to earn profits or distribute surplus among members.
questions.topic: Introduction to Not-for-Profit Organisations

questions.q: A not-for-profit organisation is required to make a return of donations received from a donor. Which of the following is the correct procedure?
opts: (A) The return of donation is to be made to the donor on the same day
(B) The return of donation is to be made within 30 days of receipt
(C) The return of donation is not required
(D) The return of donation is to be made to the donor within 7 days of receipt
questions.explanation: A not-for-profit organisation is required to make a return of donations received from a donor within 30 days of receipt.
questions.topic: Receipts and Payments Account of a Not-for-Profit Organisation

questions.q: Which of the following is not a type of not-for-profit organisation?
opts: (A) Trust
(B) Society
(C) Partnership firm
(D) Company
questions.explanation: A partnership firm is a type of business organisation, not a not-for-profit organisation.
questions.topic: Types of Not-for-Profit Organisations

questions.q: What is the purpose of preparing a Receipts and Payments Account for a not-for-profit organisation?
opts: (A) To show the financial position of the organisation
(B) To show the revenue and expenses of the organisation
(C) To show the receipts and payments of the organisation
(D) To show the profit or loss of the organisation
questions.explanation: The purpose of preparing a Receipts and Payments Account for a not-for-profit organisation is to show the receipts and payments of the organisation.
questions.topic: Receipts and Payments Account of a Not-for-Profit Organisation

questions.q: A not-for-profit organisation receives a donation of Rs. 1,00,000 from a donor. Which of the following is the correct treatment?
opts: (A) The donation is to be treated as revenue
(B) The donation is to be treated as capital
(C) The donation is to be treated as a liability
(D) The donation is to be treated as an asset
questions.explanation: A donation received from a donor is to be treated as an asset, not as revenue or liability.
questions.topic: Receipts and Payments Account of a Not-for-Profit Organisation

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a society?
opts: (A) Unlimited liability
(B) No liability of members
(C) Limited liability
(D) Liability of members is not limited
questions.explanation: The liability of members in a society is not limited, making it a characteristic of a society.
questions.topic: Characteristics of a Society

questions.q: What is the purpose of preparing a Budget for a not-for-profit organisation?
opts: (A) To show the financial position of the organisation
(B) To show the revenue and expenses of the organisation
(C) To show the receipts and payments of the organisation
(D) To show the financial performance of the organisation
questions.explanation: The purpose of preparing a Budget for a not-for-profit organisation is to show the financial performance of the organisation.
questions.topic: Budget and Budgetary Control

questions.q: A not-for-profit organisation has a surplus of Rs. 50,000 at the end of the year. Which of the following is the correct treatment?
opts: (A) The surplus is to be treated as revenue
(B) The surplus is to be treated as capital
(C) The surplus is to be distributed among members
(D) The surplus is to be used for future activities
questions.explanation: A surplus at the end of the year is to be used for future activities, not distributed among members.
questions.topic: Financial Statements of a Not-for-Profit Organisation

questions.q: What is the difference between a society and a company?
opts: (A) Society has unlimited liability, company has limited liability
(B) Society has limited liability, company has unlimited liability
(C) Society is registered with the government, company is registered with the government
(D) Society is taxable, company is exempt from tax
questions.explanation: A society has unlimited liability, whereas a company has limited liability.
questions.topic: Differences between a Society and a Company

questions.q: A not-for-profit organisation has a deficit of Rs. 20,000 at the end of the year. Which of the following is the correct treatment?
opts: (A) The deficit is to be treated as revenue
(B) The deficit is to be treated as capital
(C) The deficit is to be distributed among members
(D) The deficit is to be met from the accumulated funds
questions.explanation: A deficit at the end of the year is to be met from the accumulated funds, not distributed among members.
questions.topic: Financial Statements of a Not-for-Profit Organisation

questions.q: What is the purpose of preparing a Cash Flow Statement for a not-for-profit organisation?
opts: (A) To show the financial position of the organisation
(B) To show the revenue and expenses of the organisation
(C) To show the cash inflows and outflows of the organisation
(D) To show the profit or loss of the organisation
questions.explanation: The purpose of preparing a Cash Flow Statement for a not-for-profit organisation is to show the cash inflows and outflows of the organisation.
questions.topic: Cash Flow Statement of a Not-for-Profit Organisation

questions.q: A not-for-profit organisation has a number of fixed assets that are to be depreciated over their useful life. Which of the following is the correct method of depreciation?
opts: (A) Straight-line method
(B) Diminishing balance method
(C) Written-down value method
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: A not-for-profit organisation can use any of the three methods of depreciation, which are the straight-line method, diminishing balance method, and written-down value method.
questions.topic: Depreciation and Provision for Bad and Doubtful Debts

questions.q: A not-for-profit organisation has a number of assets that have suffered a decline in value. Which of the following is the correct treatment?
opts: (A) The decline in value is to be treated as a loss
(B) The decline in value is to be treated as a gain
(C) The decline in value is to be treated as a depreciation
(D) The decline in value is to be ignored
questions.explanation: A decline in value of assets is to be treated as a loss, not as a gain or depreciation.
questions.topic: Depreciation and Provision for Bad and Doubtful Debts

questions.q: A not-for-profit organisation is required to prepare a Balance Sheet at the end of the year. Which of the following is the correct format?
opts: (A) Assets = Capital + Surplus
(B) Assets = Capital - Surplus
(C) Assets = Capital + Liabilities
(D) Assets = Capital - Liabilities
questions.explanation: A Balance Sheet of a not-for-profit organisation is to be prepared in the format of Assets = Capital + Liabilities.
questions.topic: Financial Statements of a Not-for-Profit Organisation

questions.q: A not-for-profit organisation has a number of liabilities that are to be paid off over a period of time. Which of the following is the correct method of amortisation?
opts: (A) Straight-line method
(B) Diminishing balance method
(C) Written-down value method
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: A not-for-profit organisation can use the straight-line method for amortising liabilities.
questions.topic: Amortisation of Liabilities

questions.q: A not-for-profit organisation has a number of assets that are to be sold off. Which of the following is the correct treatment?
opts: (A) The proceeds of sale are to be treated as revenue
(B) The proceeds of sale are to be treated as capital
(C) The proceeds of sale are to be treated as a gain
(D) The proceeds of sale are to be treated as a loss
questions.explanation: The proceeds of sale of assets are to be treated as a gain, not as revenue or capital.
questions.topic: Financial Statements of a Not-for-Profit Organisation

questions.q: A not-for-profit organisation has a number of liabilities that are to be written off. Which of the following is the correct treatment?
opts: (A) The liability is to be treated as a loss
(B) The liability is to be treated as a gain
(C) The liability is to be ignored
(D) The liability is to be written off against the accumulated funds
questions.explanation: A liability that is to be written off is to be written off against the accumulated funds, not treated as a loss or gain.
questions.topic: Amortisation of Liabilities

questions.q: A not-for-profit organisation has a number of assets that are to be revalued. Which of the following is the correct treatment?
opts: (A) The revaluation is to be treated as a gain
(B) The revaluation is to be treated as a loss
(C) The revaluation is to be ignored
(D) The revaluation is to be treated as a capital item
questions.explanation: A revaluation of assets is to be treated as a loss, not as a gain or capital item.
questions.topic: Revaluation of Assets

questions.q: A not-for-profit organisation has a number of liabilities that are to be revalued. Which of the following is the correct treatment?
opts: (A) The revaluation is to be treated as a gain
(B) The revaluation is to be treated as a loss
(C) The revaluation is to be ignored
(D) The revaluation is to be treated as a capital item
questions.explanation: A revaluation of liabilities is to be treated as a gain, not as a loss or capital item.
questions.topic: Revaluation of Liabilities
